countries.name: United States
countries.notes: USCIS requires continuous residence. Any single trip exceeding 6 months (180 days) may break continuous residence. Trips of 1 year or more will disrupt eligibility.

countries.name: United Kingdom
countries.notes: For British citizenship, applicants must not have spent more than 90 days outside the UK in the 12 months before applying, and no more than 450 days total over the 5-year qualifying period.

countries.name: Canada
countries.notes: Must be physically present in Canada for at least 1,095 days (3 years) out of the 5 years before applying. Maximum 635 days abroad over the entire 5-year window.

countries.name: Australia
countries.notes: Must be physically present in Australia for 4 years before applying (including 1 year as a permanent resident). Maximum 365 days abroad total over the 4-year period.

countries.name: Germany
countries.notes: Generally requires 5 years of lawful habitual residence (reduced to 3 for special integration). Absences exceeding 6 months within a year may interrupt the qualifying period.
